name: AnimeThemesSync
description:
Automatically download OP/ED videos from AnimeThemes.moe to upgrade your collection!
Key Features: - Precise Matching: Uses AniList/MyAnimeList IDs to ensure the correct anime. - Smart Tagging: Automatically adds season tags like 'Winter 2024'. - Direct Links: Quick access to the source pages on AnimeThemes.moe.
overview: Automatically downloads anime OP/ED themes from AnimeThemes.moe for your Jellyfin library.

versions.changelog:
v1.2.1
Fix AnimeThemes Browser season matching consistency.
Season Finder now uses the same async AnimeThemes and AniList relation resolution path as Library Browser, so Auto, Direct, Series-level, and Manual states no longer disagree between the two views.
Season 0 and Specials folders are excluded from Season Finder and normal Library Browser season groups because AnimeThemes does not model Jellyfin specials seasons as separate season records.
The Finder UI now shows Needs match only when a season is actually unresolved, including cases where a resolved match has no AnimeThemes slug but does have resolved IDs or anime metadata.
Validation: dotnet test Jellyfin.Plugin.AnimeThemesSync.Tests/Jellyfin.Plugin.AnimeThemesSync.Tests.csproj --no-restore; dotnet build Emby.Plugin.AnimeThemesSync/Emby.Plugin.AnimeThemesSync.csproj --no-restore; git diff --check.

## Changes
- Add a plugin setting to enable or disable season-level theme downloads.
- Keep saved season mappings while skipping season output when season downloads are disabled.
- Publish the Jellyfin catalog thumbnail from GitHub Pages so Jellyfin can fetch it reliably.
- Remove local developer notes, IDE settings, and large sample files from the public repository.
- Expand ignore rules for generated artifacts, private notes, and AI instruction files.
Separate Jellyfin test port in VSCode tasks
